message:
feat(dashboard): return PostQuitAction to attach tmux session after zoom

The dashboard's zoom feature previously fired tmux.ZoomPane and
immediately quit, discarding its error and never re-attaching the
client to the target session. This left the user's terminal detached
after zooming into a pane.

Changes:
- Add PostQuitAction struct to carry post-exit intent (AttachSession)
  from the TUI model back to the CLI caller.
- Change dashboard.Run() signature to return (*PostQuitAction, error)
  so the caller can act on it.
- In runDashboard(), after Run() returns, call tmux.AttachOrSwitch when
  the action requests session attachment.
- Handle tmux.ZoomPane errors explicitly: surface failures in the
  health message bar instead of silently ignoring them.
- Add early return (tea.Cmd nil) in the keyboard zoom path when no
  valid pane is selected, preventing a no-op quit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
